reasoning: The question asks whether DuPont's (DD) market close price on four future dates will be higher than its close price on April 13, 2025. Between April 3 and April 13, the stock appears to have undergone a corporate action (like a stock split or a major dividend) that adjusted historical prices by a factor of ~2.7, changing the pre-split unadjusted close of 75.25 on April 3 to a post-split adjusted equivalent of around 27.78, which has since traded down to 24.14 as of April 11. Since we are comparing future prices to the April 13 close price (which is essentially the April 11 post-split close of 24.14), this is a standard stock price direction forecast. Short-term stock movements approximate a random walk, thus hovering very close to 50% for near-term resolutions. Over longer horizons (1-6 months), the equity risk premium slightly skews the probability of a positive return above 50%. Therefore, probabilities gradually increase from 50% to roughly 55%.
